message:
Add fwc serve-mcp command with stdio JSON-RPC transport and connector-to-MCP bridge

New serve-mcp subcommand: exposes discovered flywheel connectors as MCP
tools via newline-delimited JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, enabling any MCP
client (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) to invoke connector operations
directly through the standard MCP protocol.

fwc main.rs (+363 lines):
- Added ServeMcp command variant with --connector and --zone args
- execute_passthrough_command() routes serve-mcp before normal dispatch
- execute_serve_mcp() loads DiscoveryCatalog, filters connectors by
  optional selector and zone, builds MCP server state from discovered
  operations, and runs the async stdio transport loop
- Added "fwc serve-mcp github" to CLI help examples

serve_mcp.rs (+282 lines):
- state_from_connectors(): builds McpServerState from discovered
  connectors by converting operations to MCP tool definitions via
  export_tools::to_mcp_tool()
- run_stdio_transport(): async newline-delimited JSON reader/writer
  loop over generic AsyncBufRead/AsyncWrite, handling initialize,
  tools/list, tools/call, and unknown methods
- tool_handler callback pattern allows the CLI to reuse existing
  invoke planning path for tool execution
- Proper JSON-RPC error responses for malformed requests, unknown
  tools, and transport failures

Cargo.toml: added tokio dependency for async stdio I/O in serve-mcp.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
